mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community instances on Kubernetes. It's useful for developers looking to run MongoDB in a Kubernetes environment without the need for enterprise features. However, note that this operator is deprecated, and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official MongoDB Helm repo.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MongoDB Community Operator using Helm in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a namespace for MongoDB resources.
> Installs the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB custom resource to deploy a MongoDB instance.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  podSpec:
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "1Gi"
EOF

```

> Creates a MongoDB ReplicaSet with 3 members.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  podSpec:
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "1Gi"
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the MongoDB deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Lists all MongoDB instances in the mongodb namespace.
> Lists all pods in the mongodb namespace to check their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all MongoDB instances and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstalls the operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your MongoDB data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Backup current MongoDB resources.
> Upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator/master/deploy/crds/mongodb.com_mongodb_crd.yaml' to reinstall the CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB pods may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests in the MongoDB custom resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MongoDB is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB instance may not have completed its initialization.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the MongoDB pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n mongodb' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to MongoDB'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the MongoDB service is created and check the service type.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MongoDB version 5.0.3, improved logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11, ensure to check the new resource specifications in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB instance listed with 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb'.

        memory: "1Gi"
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
